# VERIFY: #66 — Live work-path tracking with session resume for agent continuity
<!-- Generated by start-feature-planning | Plan sub-agent output -->

**Intent:** Verify that the work-path tracking system enables a new session agent to know exactly where work is and what is next by reading one artifact, with live updates, multi-agent safety, and backward compatibility with the existing STM model.

---

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Running `/resume-work 66` in a new session reads `work-state.md` and presents: current tasks (with status), next actionable task, last decisions made, evidence produced — without reading any other STM file
- [ ] A cold-start (new Claude Code session, branch `feature/66-...`) automatically injects work-state context via SessionStart hook before the first user prompt
- [ ] A task marked complete via `TaskUpdate` triggers the `TaskCompleted` hook, which appends a state update to `work-state.md` within 1 second (async, non-blocking to agent)
- [ ] Running `/compact` or auto-compaction triggers the `PreCompact` hook, which commits the latest `work-state.md` to Git before context is lost
- [ ] Two agents updating different tasks concurrently do not corrupt `work-state.md` (append-only updates are monotonic and safe under last-write-wins)
- [ ] `work-state.md` is created by `start-feature-planning` after `tasks.md` is written — no manual step required
- [ ] `work-state.md` lives at `.meridian/{issue}/work-state.md`, consistent with ADR 008's issue-centric hierarchy
- [ ] Injected SessionStart context is under 1,000 tokens (verified by counting tokens in the hook output)
- [ ] Existing plays (`commit-code`, `start-feature`, `create-pr`) continue to work unchanged — no breaking changes to existing STM structure or play contracts
- [ ] Hook scripts are delivered via `track-work-state` skill and deployed by `/sync-claude` — no manual hook installation required

---

## Verification Steps

| Step | Method | Expected Outcome |
|------|--------|-----------------|
| 1. Planning creates work-state | Run `/start-feature-planning 66`, verify `.meridian/66/work-state.md` exists after plan approval | File exists with all tasks from `tasks.md`, all in `pending` status, next task identified |
| 2. SessionStart injection | Open a new Claude Code session on the issue branch, check conversation for injected context | Work-state summary appears in context before first user prompt |
| 3. Task completion updates state | Mark a task complete via `TaskUpdate`, wait 1s, read `work-state.md` | Task status changed from `in_progress` to `completed`, `last_updated` timestamp updated |
| 4. PreCompact commits state | Trigger `/compact`, check `git log` | A commit exists with message `chore(stm): work-state sync for #66` |
| 5. Cold-start resume | Close session, open new session, run `/resume-work 66` | Play reads `work-state.md` only and outputs complete context in under 5 seconds |
| 6. Multi-agent safety test | Run two agents concurrently marking different tasks complete | Both updates appear in `work-state.md`, no data corruption, both task statuses correct |
| 7. Stale state detection | Set `last_updated` to 48h ago in work-state, run `/resume-work 66` | Play warns about stale state, offers to rebuild from `tasks.md` and checkpoint history |
| 8. Context size check | Run `wc -c` on hook output or count tokens | Injected context is under 1,000 tokens |
| 9. No breaking changes | Run existing `commit-code` play on a clean branch | Play completes without errors; no changes to evidence/checkpoint artifacts |
| 10. Sync-claude deploys hook | Run `/sync-claude`, check `~/.claude/settings.json` for hook entries | `SessionStart` and `PreCompact` hooks are registered; hook script exists at `~/.claude/hooks/meridian-session.sh` |

---

## Edge Cases

| Scenario | Expected Behavior |
|----------|------------------|
| `work-state.md` missing on cold-start (never created) | `resume-work` detects absence, rebuilds from `tasks.md` + checkpoint history, writes work-state, presents result |
| `work-state.md` exists but `tasks.md` is newer (re-planned after work started) | `resume-work` detects mismatch (task count differs), warns user, offers merge or full rebuild |
| All tasks completed (work is done) | work-state shows 0 open tasks, recommends running `/create-pr` |
| Issue has no `tasks.md` (only `start-feature` was run, not `start-feature-planning`) | `resume-work` detects absent planning artifacts, informs user that planning is required first |
| Hook script missing (not deployed) | `resume-work` detects missing hook, prints setup instructions, functions in manual mode (no auto-update) |
| Task completed by agent that wasn't in `tasks.md` (ad-hoc task) | Hook appends it as an unplanned completion; `resume-work` flags it for review |
| Multi-agent: two agents complete the SAME task simultaneously | Last write wins on monotonic state (completed → completed is a no-op); no corruption |
| PreCompact hook script fails (e.g., Git error) | Hook exits non-zero (non-blocking), logs error to stderr (visible in verbose mode), session continues |
| Context window injection exceeds 1K tokens (very large task set) | Hook truncates to first 5 open tasks + summary line, logs full path to work-state for manual read |
